LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE- THE COURT GRANTED THE PETITION TO THE EXTENT FOR THE CITY TO DO THE FOLLOWING 1) REQUIRED TO DETERMINE WHETHER IT HAS DOCUMENTS RELATING TO STRUCTUS REVIEW AND PROVIDE THOSE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO THE PETITIONERS REQUEST. 2) WITH RESPECT TO THE 9 PAGES DESCRIBED IN THE FLOODING THAT ARE LODGE IN THE PERSONNEL FILE, THE CITY TO REVIEW THOSE DOCUMENTS AND MAKE ITS OWN DETERMINATION TO REDACT THIS DOCUMENT STRIKING INDIVIDUAL NAMES LEAVING THE DESCRIPTION OF THE EVENT WITHOUT COMPROMISING THE ANONYMITY OF THE EMPLOYEE AND TO PRODUCE THOSE DOCUMENTS AND IF IT CANNOT DO SO TO SUBMIT THE 9 PAGES IN CAMERA FOR COURT'S DETERMINATION, EXCEPT IN THOSE REGARD THE PETITION IS DENIED. MISS DELOREY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Feb-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees IS GRANTED. FEES ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $25,200.00, WHICH THE COURT DETERMINES TO BE THE REASONABLE FEES FOR TIME REASONABLY SPENT ON THE CASE. COSTS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,059.13. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Maura Baldocchi, CSR# 5207 =301/PJB/AL

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Petitioner Mitchell Enginnering, Inc.; From Taking The Deposition Of Third Parties Patrick Collins And Environmental Construction Solutions, Inc. And For Sanctions Against Mitchell Engineering, Inc. And Its Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Catherine Delorey, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Mitchell Engineering
David Norman, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: City & County of S.F.
Joan Martin, CSR # 6036

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Respondent City And County Of San Francisco's and Non-Party Witnesses Patrick Collins And Environmental & Construction Solutions, Inc.'s Motion For Protective Order To Prevent The Taking Of The Depositions Of Third Parties Patrick Collins And Environmental & Construction Solutions, Inc. is granted. There is no proceeding in which to take discovery.
Petitioner's objections to the declaration of David L. Norman are overruled. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Hearing On Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Oct-04-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. CATHERINE W. DELOREY OF MCINERNEY & DILLON, TEL. NO. (510) 465-7100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. DAVID L. NORMAN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3989 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; JUDGE BUSCH DISCLOSED THAT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, IS A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY AND NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE- THE COURT GRANTED THE PETITION TO THE EXTENT FOR THE CITY TO DO THE FOLLOWING 1) REQUIRED TO DETERMINE WHETHER IT HAS DOCUMENTS RELATING TO STRUCTUS REVIEW AND PROVIDE THOSE DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO THE PETITIONERS REQUEST. 2) WITH RESPECT TO THE 9 PAGES DESCRIBED IN THE FLOODING THAT ARE LODGE IN THE PERSONNEL FILE, THE CITY TO REVIEW THOSE DOCUMENTS AND MAKE IT'S OWN DETERMINATION TO REDACT THIS DOCUMENT STRIKING INDIVIDUAL NAMES LEAVING THE DESCRIPTION OF THE EVENT WITHOUT COMPROMISING THE ANONYMITY OF THE EMPLOYEE AND TO PRODUCE THOSE DOCUMENTS AND IF IT CANNOT DO SO TO SUBMIT THE 9 PAGES IN CAMERA FOR COURT'S DETERMINATION, EXCEPT IN THOSE REGARD THE PETITION IS DENIED.
